stemHtml: What is the definition of Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring (RAIM) as a system utilised by an aviation GNSS receiver? RAIM is the...

options.a: use of ground-based facilities such as VOR and/or DME to verify and enhance the accuracy of its calculated position.
options.b: exclusion of faulty satellites by means of comparing the satellite clock data and isolating satellites transmitting differing clock data.
options.c: ability of the receiver to perform a receiver clock-error computation and enhance the accuracy of the initial pseudo-ranges.
options.d: use of redundant range measurements to verify the position produced by the minimum number of satellites required for a three dimensional fix.
